rationale: Minimal, surgical change across 3 files. All modifications are identical in nature: adding `false` as the third argument to `setSize()` and removing redundant inline CSS assignments that the stylesheet already handles via `canvas { width: 100% !important; height: 100% !important; }`. No new APIs, no architectural changes, no dependency additions. The CSS-owns-layout / JS-owns-buffer-resolution separation is the correct Three.js pattern for mobile fullscreen rendering.

line_accuracy.scene.ts: Lines 44-46 and 69-71 confirmed — setSize + manual CSS at exact positions stated
line_accuracy.resize.ts: Lines 23-25 confirmed — setSize + manual CSS at exact positions stated
line_accuracy.main.ts: Lines 88-90 confirmed — setSize + manual CSS at exact positions stated
line_accuracy.style.css: Lines 23-27 confirmed — canvas rule with !important already present

architectural_impact.scope: rendering layer only — no changes to audio, input, seed, or UI modules
architectural_impact.coupling_change: reduces coupling — CSS solely owns layout, JS solely owns GPU buffer sizing
architectural_impact.api_surface: no public API changes; initScene and attachResizeHandler signatures unchanged
architectural_impact.breaking_changes: none — external consumers see identical DOM layout and rendering behavior

risks_identified.risk: Three.js setSize(w, h, false) skips inline style writes — relies entirely on CSS for canvas dimensions
risks_identified.mitigation: CSS rule `canvas { width: 100% !important; height: 100% !important; }` already exists and uses !important, guaranteeing layout regardless of any inline styles

test_coverage_assessment: Two new test files cover all modified code paths. Tests verify the `false` third argument, absence of inline styles, pixel ratio calculations, orientation/viewport event handling, and context restore behavior. Coverage is proportionate to change scope.
